text: Stepping through the glass doors into this brightly lit space, I am struck by a sensation of entering a threshold—one that divides the sterile exterior from a vibrant interior dedicated to inquiry and innovation. The bold letters R+D announce a domain of research and development. The color and lights enliven my mind. I feel intellectually invigorated, eager to participate in the exchange of curiosity and knowledge that seems to pulse through this environment. Yet, I am also aware of a gentle unease—the sense that, like the technological wonders exhibited here, I am both observer and subject under scrutiny, a being traversing the border between what is known and what is possible.
